channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Are male voters reluctant to vote for a woman? Harris' backers are confronting the question head on
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The concern has been there all along, but now it's being talked about openly: Are some men reluctant to vote for Democrat Kamala Harris because she’s a woman? The vice president rarely references her gender on the campaign trail, but her key supporters are starting to make more dir
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trial on hold for New Jersey man charged in knife attack that injured Salman Rushdie
item.description: The attempted murder trial of the man charged with injuring author Salman Rushdie in a 2022 knife attack in New York has been put on hold.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Residents slog through flooded streets, clear debris after Hurricane Milton tore through Florida
item.description: LITHIA, Fla. (AP) — Florida residents slogged through flooded streets, gathered up scattered debris and assessed damage to their homes on Friday after Hurricane Milton smashed through coastal communities and spawned a barrage of deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS O'MEARA, BRENDAN FARRINGTON and TY O'NEIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An elevator malfunction killed 1 and trapped 12 at a Colorado tourist mine
item.description: Denver (AP) — Investigators on Friday were trying to figure out what led to an elevator accident inside a former Colorado gold mine that killed a tour guide, injured four others and left 12 people trapped for hours at the bottom of the tourist attraction 1,000 feet (305 meters) beneath the surface.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Back-to-back hurricanes reshape 2024 campaign’s final stretch
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A pair of unwelcome and destructive guests named Helene and Milton have stormed their way into this year's presidential election.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 3 in 5 millennials want full access to abortion: Study
item.description: The largest percentile of individuals who do not want to live in a "fully legal" abortion state is Gen Z at 33%, followed by Gen X and Baby Boomers (30%).
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: If you let your flood insurance lapse and then got hit by Helene, you may be able to renew it
item.description: Residents in states hit by Hurricane Helene who had coverage through the federal flood insurance program but let it lapse before the storm hit may be able to renew it and still be covered from the impact.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oregon's most populous county adds gas utility to $51B climate suit against fossil fuel companies
item.description: Oregon's Multnomah County, home to Portland, has added the state's largest natural gas utility to its $51.5 billion climate lawsuit against fossil fuel companies.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: U.S. expands sanctions on Iran in response to its ballistic attack on Israel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. on Friday announced new sanctions on Iran's energy sector in response to its Oct. 1 attack on Israel when it fired roughly 180 missiles into the country.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know as Florida residents begin cleaning up after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Cleanup from Hurricane Milton was underway Friday as residents returned home after the storm barreled across Florida, spawning tornadoes, knocking out power to millions, damaging homes and leading to hundreds of rescues.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 7-Eleven to close over 400 'underperforming' locations in North America
item.description: The company cited slow sales, declining traffic, inflationary pressures, and a decrease in cigarette purchases among the reasons why some stores are closing.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jamie Dimon: 'Treacherous' geopolitical conditions getting worse
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon on Friday warned about potential risks in the future for the economy, citing the war in Ukraine and Israel's war against Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: US sees a window for a new push to break political deadlock in Lebanon to ease conflict
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With Israel’s sabotage and military operations in Lebanon taking out many of Hezbollah’s senior leaders, some in Washington and elsewhere believe there may be a window for a new push to break the political deadlock in Lebanon to try to ease escalating war.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: New Bryan Kohberger trial date, Gabby Petito bodycam video, Epstein abuse
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida Gov. DeSantis helps Waffle House employees cook for first responders
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis put on the gloves and picked up a spatula at a Sarasota-area Waffle House on Thursday to help cook for first responders after Hurricane Milton left a path of destruction across the Tampa Bay area.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Satellite images show before and after Hurricane Milton along Gulf Coast
item.description: Satellite images showcases the widespread damage and destruction left behind in Florida after Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: Border Report Live: How Mexico water woes impact the US border
item.description: From contamination on the west coast to a drying up Rio Grande in Texas, water woes stretch across and along the U.S.-Mexico border.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Climate change gave significant boost to Milton's destructive power, scientists say
item.description: Researchers said that without climate change, a hurricane like Milton would make landfall as a weaker Category 2, not considered a “major” storm, instead of a Category 3.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 killed, 23 rescued after equipment malfunction at tourist gold mine in Colorado
item.description: One person was killed and 23 were rescued after being trapped for about six hours inside a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: This winter may be less predictable than the last few. Here's why
item.description: This winter was originally thought to be dominated by the La Niña phenomenon. But now, the Climate Prediction Center believes it will be a "weak and a short duration La Niña" – if it appears at all.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Abortion has passed inflation as the top election issue for women under 30, survey finds
item.description: A survey has found that abortion has passed inflation to become the top issue in the presidential election for women younger than 30.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House Democrats bet on small and fun events for minority outreach
item.description: The House Democratic campaign arm is increasingly relying on small, community-centered events for its get-out-the-vote efforts among voters of color, looking to increase interest with nonpolitical hooks.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: Massive hole swallows 2 homes in Florida
item.description: The wild scene raised concerns about whether the ground collapse was caused by a sinkhole or water damage from sewage issues.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, ABC 4

item.title: 1 person dies and 12 are rescued after elevator malfunctions at Colorado gold mine tourist site
item.description: DENVER (AP) — One person was killed and 12 people were rescued after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Houston chemical plant leak kills 2, injures at least 35
item.description: A chemical leak in Deer Park Texas has left two dead and at least 35 more injured at the PEMEX oil refinery Thursday night, according to the Deer Park Office of Emergency Management.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1 killed, dozens rescued after mine equipment malfunction near Cripple Creek
item.description: Workers successfully rescued 23 people on Thursday from a former Colorado gold mine, now operated as a tourist site, including 12 people who were trapped about 1,000 feet underground, officials announced late Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane misinformation causing misery for survivors: Abrams
item.description: Radio caller tells Dan Abrams a relative believes that if he accepts hurricane relief from FEMA, the government could take his house.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Internet Archive data breach exposes more than 31 million user accounts: reports
item.description: The Internet Archive, a popular digital library known for its Wayback Machine, was hacked and suffered a breach that reportedly exposed the data of 31 million user accounts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa sees 5x the usual October rainfall due to Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton's landfall as a Category 3 hurricane didn't just bring devastating storm surge and extremely dangerous wind speeds, it also left Tampa Bay soaked with historic rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, a dangerous storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm. It weakened as it plowed through Florida late Wednesday into Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A former DEA agent is convicted of protecting drug traffickers
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A former U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration agent in Buffalo, New York, was convicted of corruption Thursday following a second trial on charges he used his position to protect drug traffickers he believed were associated with organized crime.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-Israel agitators deface Harvard statute day after Oct. 7 anniversary: ‘Bring the war home’
item.description: Just after the one­-year anniversary of the Oct. 7 massacre in Israel, demonstrators vandalized Harvard's campus in "an act of solidarity with the Palestinian resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. reacts to his ‘fearless’ mother Ethel’s passing: ‘Invented tough love’
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. took to social media on Thursday to commemorate the life of his late mother, Ethel Kennedy, in a heartfelt post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: A federal judge rejects a call to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she won't order the presidential battleground state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections despite recent disruptions to registration caused by Hurricane Helene. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Milton spawned high number of destructive, deadly tornadoes
item.description: FT. PIERCE, Fla. (AP) — Dozens of tornadoes spawned by Hurricane Milton caught many Floridians by surprise as they braced for heavy rain, strong winds and storm surges. Violent twisters were seen crossing highways, ripping off roofs and downing trees and power lines.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, ISABELLA O'MALLEY and SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fisher-Price recalls over 2 million infant swings after 5 deaths reported
item.description: A recall has been issued for more than 2 million Fisher-Price infant swings after five deaths were reported.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, Fox 5
